summary: The diff implements most of the required features: backend entityType model extension, unified login landing page, dedicated login pages for all four entity types, hotel dashboard, updated RoleGuard and useRoleBasedAccess hook, and updated App.tsx navigation. One notable gap is the admin-only endpoint rejection for non-admin principals on the backend. The hotel dashboard route protection relies on RoleGuard but explicit wrapping is not confirmed in the truncated diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Admin-only endpoints reject calls from non-admin principals
missing_requirements.reason: The backend/main.mo diff shows the EntityType and UserProfile model changes and includes MixinAuthorization, but no explicit admin-role check that rejects non-admin callers on protected endpoints is visible in the truncated diff.

missing_requirements.requirement: Non-hotel users are redirected to /login/hotel when accessing /hotel-dashboard
missing_requirements.reason: HotelDashboard.tsx does not show explicit use of RoleGuard wrapping the component in the truncated diff, and the route-level guard configuration in App.tsx is not fully visible to confirm RoleGuard with allowedRoles=['hotel'] is applied to the /hotel-dashboard route.

hallucinated_features.description: ProfileSetupModal includes a role/entityType selector allowing users to pick any entity type (hotel, ngo, volunteer, admin) during generic profile setup
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request specifies that entity type should be set automatically based on which login page the user uses, not via a generic profile modal with a dropdown. This modal-based role selection was not requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Data migration module that converts all existing user profiles to entityType hotel by default
hallucinated_features.reason: No migration of existing data was requested. The build request only asked to add the entityType field to the model and expose endpoints. Migrating all existing profiles to hotel type was not specified.
